title: StockCharts CCI +100/-100 trend regime, adapted to NIFTY daily long/cash
data_required: Daily OHLC; CCI(20) from typical price and mean deviation.
signal_rule: CCI(20) crosses above +100 after prior close; exit signal when CCI(20) crosses below -100.
entry_timing: Enter next session open after +100 bullish CCI cross.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after -100 bearish CCI cross.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
why: StockCharts documents CCI above +100 as emerging strength/uptrend and below -100 as weakness; fixed thresholds make it backtestable.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 7.35%, total 116.39%, max DD -20.04%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 9.87%, total 178.53%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject/not deployment-ready: underperforms same-span NIFTY buy-and-hold after costs.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; NIFTY index proxy is not directly tradable; next-open execution from prior-close signal; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship/proxy/execution/sample-length caveats apply. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: StockCharts Slow Stochastic oversold bullish cross / overbought bearish cross on NIFTY
data_required: Daily OHLC; Slow Stochastic %K/%D (14,3,3).
signal_rule: Slow %K crosses above Slow %D while both are below 20; exit when Slow %K crosses below Slow %D while both are above 80.
entry_timing: Enter next session open after oversold bullish cross.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after overbought bearish cross.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
why: StockCharts defines stochastic oscillator, signal-line crosses, and 20/80 oversold/overbought zones; this uses only objective crossings.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 2.24%, total 27.19%, max DD -38.22%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 9.87%, total 178.53%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject/not deployment-ready: underperforms same-span NIFTY buy-and-hold after costs.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; NIFTY index proxy is not directly tradable; next-open execution from prior-close signal; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship/proxy/execution/sample-length caveats apply. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: StockCharts Keltner Channel upper-band breakout, adapted to NIFTY daily long/cash
data_required: Daily OHLC; EMA(20), ATR(10), Keltner bands at EMA ± 2×ATR.
signal_rule: Close crosses above upper Keltner Channel; exit when close crosses below EMA(20) middle line.
entry_timing: Enter next session open after upper-band breakout close.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after close crosses below middle EMA.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
why: StockCharts describes Keltner Channels as EMA plus/minus ATR bands and upper-band moves as strength/breakout context; the entry/exit are fully mechanical.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 2.41%, total 29.58%, max DD -18.03%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 9.87%, total 178.53%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject/not deployment-ready: underperforms same-span NIFTY buy-and-hold after costs.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; NIFTY index proxy is not directly tradable; next-open execution from prior-close signal; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship/proxy/execution/sample-length caveats apply. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: StockCharts Parabolic SAR reversal, adapted to NIFTY daily long/cash proxy
data_required: Daily OHLC index; Parabolic SAR with AF step 0.02 and max 0.20.
signal_rule: Parabolic SAR flips from above price to below price on prior daily bar (bullish reversal).
entry_timing: Enter next session open after bullish SAR flip.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after SAR flips from below price to above price.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 2.17%, total 25.63%, max DD -29.22%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 10.14%, total 178.96%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Needs more work: lower drawdown but return trails same-span benchmark.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; index proxy is not directly tradable; execution requires NIFTY futures/ETF mapping; next-open fills from prior-close signals; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship is lower for index than stock universe but proxy/execution/sample-length caveats remain. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: StockCharts RSI oversold rebound, adapted to NIFTY daily long/cash proxy
data_required: Daily OHLC index; Wilder RSI(14).
signal_rule: RSI(14) crosses back above 30 after being below 30 on prior daily close (oversold rebound).
entry_timing: Enter next session open after RSI crosses above 30.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after RSI crosses below 70 from above; force-exit at final close if still open.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 2.42%, total 28.84%, max DD -33.19%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 10.14%, total 178.96%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Needs more work: lower drawdown but return trails same-span benchmark.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; index proxy is not directly tradable; execution requires NIFTY futures/ETF mapping; next-open fills from prior-close signals; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship is lower for index than stock universe but proxy/execution/sample-length caveats remain. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: StockCharts moving-average golden/death cross, adapted to NIFTY index proxy
data_required: Daily OHLC index; 50-DMA and 200-DMA.
signal_rule: 50-DMA crosses above 200-DMA (golden cross) on prior close data.
entry_timing: Enter next session open after the golden-cross signal.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after 50-DMA crosses below 200-DMA (death cross).
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
source_note: StockCharts documents moving-average crossovers and golden/death cross interpretation; tested as a fixed NIFTY trend-following rule.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 3.79%, total 45.28%, max DD -42.47%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 11.32%, total 193.62%, max DD -38.44%.

title: StockCharts Bollinger Band oversold reversion, adapted to NIFTY index proxy
data_required: Daily OHLC index; 20-DMA and 20-day standard deviation bands at ±2σ.
signal_rule: Close is below the lower 20-day Bollinger Band (20-DMA - 2σ) on prior close data.
entry_timing: Enter next session open after lower-band oversold close.
exit_timing: Exit next session open after close recovers above the 20-DMA middle band.
sizing_risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
source_note: StockCharts documents Bollinger Bands as volatility envelopes and lower-band oversold context; tested with a fixed middle-band exit.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 3.65%, total 47.19%, max DD -38.10%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 10.00%, total 179.63%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject / not deployment-ready: underperforms same-span benchmark.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-07-27→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; index proxy is not directly tradable; execution requires NIFTY futures/ETF mapping; next-open fills from prior-close signals; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; survivorship/proxy/sample-length caveats. Source HTTP 200 verified.

title: Faber 10-month SMA trend-following timing, adapted to NIFTY
data_required: Daily/monthly OHLC index; 10-month SMA
signal_rule: risk-on when prior month-end close is above its 10-month SMA; risk-off otherwise.
entry_timing: enter at next available session open for the following month after risk-on signal.
exit_timing: exit at next available session open for the following month after risk-off signal.
sizing_risk: 100% NIFTY exposure when risk-on, cash when risk-off; no leverage.
costs_slippage: 5 bps per side or turnover, as specified in rulebook
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 3.70%, total 47.91%, max DD -43.72%; same-span benchmark CAGR 10.04%, total 180.54%, max DD -37.77%.
verdict: Reject / defensive-only, not return-ready.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-07-31→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; index proxy/cash return simplification; ignores tax and futures/ETF roll/cash yield; monthly execution assumes next-open fills; sample starts after 10-month warmup.

title: QuantInsti 52-week-high effect, adapted to NIFTY50 stocks
data_required: Daily OHLC for stocks; 252-day highs; monthly rebalance
signal_rule: at first trading day of each month, select stocks whose prior close is within 5% of the prior 252-session high (close >= 0.95 × 252-day high).
entry_timing: buy selected stocks equal-weight at that month’s first session open.
exit_timing: hold for the month; square off/rebalance at next month’s first session open.
sizing_risk: equal-weight active names; no leverage; cash if no names pass.
costs_slippage: 5 bps per side or turnover, as specified in rulebook
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 17.14%, total 95.29%, max DD -16.07%; same-span benchmark CAGR 18.23%, total 103.05%, max DD -17.23%.
verdict: Reject / not deployment-ready.
caveats: Data coverage 2022-02-16→2026-05-11; OHLC filtered rows 0; local NIFTY50 cache can have survivorship/constituent-history bias; 5% near-high threshold is a fixed implementation choice for the source “near 52-week high” condition; no liquidity/impact/tax model; benchmark equal-weight cached stocks same span.
rules:
Market fit: India-direct / India-adapted from QuantInsti NSE article
Instrument/universe: local cached NIFTY50 stock daily OHLC basket
Data required: daily OHLC for stocks; rolling 252-session high; monthly rebalance calendar.
Signal rule: at first trading day of each month, select stocks whose prior close is within 5% of the prior 252-session high (close >= 0.95 × 252-day high).
Entry timing: buy selected stocks equal-weight at that month’s first session open.
Exit timing: hold for the month; square off/rebalance at next month’s first session open.
Sizing/risk: equal-weight active names; no leverage; cash if no names pass.
Costs/slippage: 5 bps on turnover.
Why systematic: monthly measurable price-to-52-week-high rule; no discretionary selection.

Exit timing: hold for the month; square off/rebalance at next month’s first session open.
Sizing/risk: equal-weight active names; no leverage; cash if no names pass.
Costs/slippage: 5 bps on turnover.
Why systematic: monthly measurable price-to-52-week-high rule; no discretionary selection.

title: NIFTY50 stock basket 30-day Bollinger lower-to-upper reversal
instrument: locally available NIFTY50 stock daily OHLC universe, equal-weight all active signals
rules: Universe/instrument: locally available NIFTY50 stock daily OHLC universe, equal-weight all active signals. Signal: 30-day Bollinger Bands, middle=SMA30, upper/lower=±2 rolling std. Entry: next session open after close crosses below lower band. Exit: next session open after close crosses above upper band. Sizing: equal-weight among active names, no leverage. Costs/slippage: 5 bps on portfolio turnover. Data: local NIFTY50 stock cache. Benchmarks over comparable span: NIFTY buy-and-hold ~9.1% CAGR / -17.2% DD; daily rebalanced equal-weight local stock universe ~20.0% CAGR / -16.9% DD.
verdict: Promising but not deployment-ready: beat local equal-weight stock buy-and-hold in this survivorship-prone sample, with similar drawdown; needs PIT constituents, liquidity checks, and train/test validation.
caveats: Survivorship/constituent cache caveat; all active signals equally weighted. Filtered bad OHLC rows across stocks: 0.

title: NIFTY 50 RSI(14) oversold-to-overbought reversal proxy
instrument: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC signal-validation proxy
rules: Universe/instrument: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC signal-validation proxy. Signal: 14-day Wilder RSI; oversold threshold 30, overbought threshold 70. Entry: next session open after RSI crosses below 30. Exit: next session open after RSI crosses above 70. Sizing: 100% notional while long. Costs/slippage: 5 bps per side. Data: local NIFTY index cache, positive OHLC validated. Benchmark: NIFTY buy-and-hold over same span returned ~179% / 9.9% CAGR / -38.4% max DD.
verdict: Reject as standalone rule: high trade win rate but very low CAGR versus buy-and-hold; strict RSI 70 exit creates long idle/holding regimes.
caveats: Index is not directly tradable; no stop/time exit added beyond sourced 30/70 thresholds. Filtered bad OHLC rows: 0.

title: NIFTY 50 50/200 SMA golden-cross trend proxy
instrument: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC signal-validation proxy
rules: Universe/instrument: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC signal-validation proxy. Signal: 50-day SMA crossing above/below 200-day SMA on close. Entry: next session open after 50SMA crosses above 200SMA. Exit: next session open after 50SMA crosses below 200SMA. Sizing: 100% notional while long. Costs/slippage: 5 bps on entry and 5 bps on exit/turnover. Data: local NIFTY index cache, positive OHLC validated. Benchmark: NIFTY buy-and-hold over same span returned ~179% / 9.9% CAGR / -38.4% max DD.
verdict: Reject as standalone index rule: it materially underperformed NIFTY buy-and-hold and had slightly worse drawdown, despite low turnover.
caveats: Index is not directly tradable; use futures/ETF execution validation. Filtered bad OHLC rows: 0. Close-derived signals fill at next open.

title: Faber 10-month SMA timing model adapted to NIFTY 50
market_fit: India adaptation of public tactical asset allocation timing rule
instrument: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC proxy, monthly signal
data_required: NIFTY daily OHLC aggregated to month-end; 10-month simple moving average of monthly closes
signal_rule: At month-end, bullish when monthly close is above its trailing 10-month SMA; bearish when monthly close is below/equal the 10-month SMA.
entry_timing: Enter at next trading session open after a bullish month-end signal when currently in cash.
exit_timing: Exit at next trading session open after a bearish month-end signal when currently long.
sizing_risk: 100% notional long NIFTY proxy while signal is bullish; cash otherwise; no leverage.
benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 6.64%, total 101.23%, max DD -21.31%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 9.89%, total 178.96%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject/not deployment-ready: positive but trails or worsens simple same-span NIFTY buy-hold.
caveats: Source HTTP 200 verified=True; OHLC filtered rows 0; NIFTY index proxy is not directly tradable; execution needs futures/ETF mapping; next-open fills from prior-close signals; 5 bps per entry/exit side; ignores taxes, impact, roll/cash yield; sample length 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; proxy/execution/sample-length caveats apply. Survivorship caveat is low for index-level proxy but still relevant if adapted to stock baskets.

benchmark_comparison: Strategy CAGR 5.00%, total 70.04%, max DD -16.46%; same-span NIFTY buy-hold CAGR 9.89%, total 178.96%, max DD -38.44%.
verdict: Reject/not deployment-ready: underperforms same-span NIFTY buy-and-hold after costs.
caveats: Data coverage 2015-06-26→2026-05-12; OHLC filtered rows 0; source HTTP 200 verified; NIFTY index proxy is not directly tradable; next-open execution from prior-close signal; 5 bps/side costs; ignores taxes, impact, futures roll/ETF tracking/cash yield; survivorship/proxy/execution/sample-length caveats apply.
rules:
Market fit: India-adaptable
Instrument/universe: NIFTY 50 index daily OHLC proxy
Data required: Daily OHLC; Aroon Up/Down(25).
Signal rule: Aroon Up(25) crosses above Aroon Down(25) and Aroon Up is above 70 on the signal close.
Entry timing: Enter next session open after the prior-close signal.
Exit timing: Exit next session open when Aroon Down crosses above Aroon Up, or Aroon Up falls below 50.
Sizing/risk: 100% notional while in position; cash otherwise; no leverage.
Costs/slippage: 5 bps per entry/exit side
Benchmark: same-span NIFTY buy-and-hold
